tokens: Okay; low-gain is set, and heading about 035...Oh, let me set this thing out of the way again.
This has been giving me more trouble.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, no readings called out (in the checklist).
And when you get going, I'll give you a little advice on what we're going to do on the way to Station 3.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, let me tell you a few things first, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Those two major kinds of blocks that we sampled there...(they were) about the (only) two varieties we saw in the area, (and) it's a long extrapolation I realize, but they do resemble in color, and I believe in texture, the blue-gray rocks and the light-tan rocks up on the Massif.
So I feel confident that - fairly confident - that we sampled at least the two major units visible from a distance in the South Massif.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think that there is some...(correcting himself) a lot of post-mission work to be done on correlating the angularity and possibly even the albedos of the rocks we sampled with those on the Massif.
We should have good pictures of both from a distance and up close.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...that extrapolation is the nature of our art.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Bob, I'm not going to...How am I on the film?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get that for you, Jack...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think you're doing fairly well, now.
And before you guys get too far, a couple of comments we want to do on the way.
There is a Rover sample stop in your checklist, it used to be at 073 and 6.3; it's the first thing there, halfway out to Hole-in-the-Wall.
And we're now going to have that Rover sample stop at 078 and 7.0.
That should be along your tracks going home.
So, about 078 and 7.0, we'll have the Rover sample stop.
And the gravimeter people have won today, and we're going to stop and get off the Rover and get a gravimeter reading at that location.
We're taking out (time from) some stop, I'm not sure quite where.
And right now, Jack, you're right-on on the film says a little note in front of me.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, we're on the top, coming off the highest lobe of the Scarp looking back into the valley.
And it's quite a scene back there, but we still cannot see the LM.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, turn a partial pan; I know it's into the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's take one from right here.
I want the whole thing (that is, the whole valley).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got a pan down the valley.
This is just going to be right into the (Sun)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Get it up as we come around.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Keep turning around over there, and I'll get that Scarp.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, you talk about a mysterious looking place.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: They can cut some frames - some parts of those pictures out - and make a nice photograph.
(Laughing) (With the) TV camera, (and) maps (in the way).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, looking at the light mantle: No more comments except that by that rake sample and just looking, there's certainly are fewer fragments than we saw at Station 2.
The main thing that we can tell about the light mantle and when we're on it, of course, is the light-colored craters.
The fresher craters all appear to be light colored.
As they get older, the albedo goes down and potentially (that can be explained if the craters) have been dusted with material from the dark mantle or from other sites.
Either that or it's just the lunar patination that we're all familiar with.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Give me that bearing and range again for the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think that that will be enough to hack it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, if not, we can go down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Stay on the road you're on.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I'm not on any road, but I'm stopping here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I thought you guys were making a road.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And the Rover numbers (roll and pitch) should be fairly flat for the ole gravimeter.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Parker) Well...That means we have to change here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, right over here to my right...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Maybe it's the best we can do, but it's still going to be on a slope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I'll level it off on a (garbled)...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: On the rim of that crater that's built up a little bit?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What's your...Can you tell your roll?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, now that's about zero (roll) right there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, this roll indicator isn't very...Oh; zero.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You'll change it (the roll) as soon as you get off.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, you got to get off anyway.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do I have to get off for this?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why should I have to get off?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: So you don't move the ole gravimeter.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) Give me your sampler 'cause that's the other thing I have to do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll get Rover samples, at least.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We want Gene and Jack both off.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, if you need me off; Jack, just punched it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: They don't know anything about your PLSS noise.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gene, we'd like both of you off.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me wait until it settles down here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This thing is all (garbled, possibly "taped shut"), isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Cycle it a couple of times.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, I don't know...Yeah, they'll change.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Say, Bob, I need a quick f-stop for the 500.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Bob, can I punch it (the gravimeter) again?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Go to Standby and then punch it again.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You don't have to wait for it to time out, do you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Geno, f-stop for the 500 millimeter should be the same as for the 70.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Jack, I presume you're getting some Rover samples here off the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Are you guys finding much in the way of rocks here?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm going to get you some instant rock out of a small pit crater...(correcting himself) pit-bottom crater.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, up to frame count 36 is the outcrop - or boulders - at the top of the South Massif.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Instant rock out of a 2-meter pit bottom crater - off the inner wall.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, let's make it 30 centimeters down from the rim.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We've got about 30 seconds left for that gravimeter reading.
You want to start finishing up your tasks and getting back toward the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And through frame count 57 are the North Massif from part of the western portions to part of the eastern portions.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; and what was that frame count?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Here's something different...Here's a little...(Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, guys, we're ready for the gravimeter reading.
And we'd like a frame count on you, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: A chunk of yellow-brown rock that apparently has several spots behind it, probably indicating direction from which it came...Oh, no...What is that?
Well, what the heck, I'll sample it anyway.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get my (low-gain) antenna set so...It's not quite...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) (You'll) probably read us better now, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did you get the other words on the 500?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, you were reading at probably a 90-degree low-gain angle.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We've been reading through the LM also.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Thirty-two Easy is just another small fragment.
(To Gene) You know what I need?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we're ready for you guys to go on at your earliest convenience.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Houston) Do you want me to load (more bags into) the LRV sampler?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Jack, is that your last LRV sample bag?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I only had one left, but it (meaning the sampler)'s loaded now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You did get the (gravimeter) reading, right, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And if you don't have one left for that sample at Hole-in-the-Wall, Jack, we'd like you to get a new set of sample bags.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) And still understand that thirty-two Echo was your last sample.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, about 2 inches below the surface here, you run into that blue-gray material down there and it's in little clods, and it breaks apart in your hands.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did you get some of that in your Rover sample?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, but I got it out of that instant rock crater.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We oughta...Just grab a quick Rover sample and we'll take off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That was a pretty interesting (laughing) episode.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, you know, we haven't been trenching like we should or we would have...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Finishing Jack's thought) We would have found it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But, really those craters are giving us the same information: that there's a light-colored material underneath.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: 17, we're ready for you guys to move on and we'd like to eliminate the Rover sample at Hole-in-the-Wall.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We got on a minute ago.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, do I understand that these Rover samples, Jack, are in your pockets?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: They're in the bag on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's light-colored soil from a depth of about...It's mixed with a little of the upper surface, but (it's) mostly light-colored soil from a depth of about 15 centimeters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Wonder what I'd do for an encore?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It looks like the light mantle in here is covered with dark to a depth of about 5 or 10 centimeters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You might want to go Min, Jack, on your diverter.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Right now, I'm sort of warm.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: When we start driving, you might want to go...I'm going to zap myself with a cold.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can do it on here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you take any pictures at all while you were there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you turn right to (garbled)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Making a right-hand turn for a pan.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me see where we're going.
You know, a little more and that hole would have been in the way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We left some of our litter.
Not a complete pan but it will show the location.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Geno, you're heading for a spot that's about 080/5.5, approximately.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, you guys following tracks home or not?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Hole-in-the-Wall should be at about 080 and maybe 5.7.
And we're not going to stop to get a Rover sample at Hole-in-the-Wall.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause, listening to Parker) Okay, that sounds reasonable because it's just nothing but lots of rolling terrain.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I think we have a good sample of only-partially-contaminated light mantle in that last Rover sample that Gene accidentally discovered was right under our feet.
It's almost certainly the light-colored material that we've been talking about in the walls of the craters.
And, as a matter of fact, that instant rock sample I took was light-colored and probably represents the same stuff, indurated slightly.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That light-colored mantle has that bluish tint that you saw in those rocks.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Parker) I still don't think there's anything...We ought to...We ought to get a core in this light mantle sometime; and probably Station 3 is going to be the place.
I hope that's still in the agenda.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Say, Bob, can you update the mileage on Station 3?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you want mileage to it or do you want the range and bearing at it?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, range and bearing at it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we're going to say about 089 and 6.1 for Station 3.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Do you want to give us another range and bearing right now?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Do you get the feeling that we're the only ones out here, Jack?
Looking around...(Responding to Parker) 073 (bearing to the SEP transmitter), 10.3 (distance driven), 6.6 (distance to the SEP transmitter).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I have a feeling that whatever darkens the...Ooh, there's a beautiful little glass-lined crater...pit-bottom crater.
Whatever darkens the light mantle is not a one-time-only mantling of darker material.
It's something that happens over a period of time, continually, because craters of all sizes and apparent degradation are darkened and there are lighter craters that are light to varying degrees.
It seems to be a continuum of albedo change.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You know that little crater on the side of the North Massif that we're thinking about going to doesn't look nearly as light-colored or haloed as it does in (overhead) pictures, does it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, (it isn't as light-colored as it seemed in the overhead pictures).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, let's see where we are, I don't want to run into that big crater at the foot of the (Scarp)...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think you're almost to the rim.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I want to go down here if I can.
My tracks are over there to the left, I haven't crossed them yet.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 073, 6.3 (bearing and range to the SEP transmitter).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, that's a sight, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know why something that's all approximately the same hue should...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The lack of color has got to contribute to the inability to judge distance.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See the lobes coming out...(See what looks like) lobes coming out from the Scarp.
The Scarp, rather than being a line in there on the plain, appears to be lobes.
I got a couple of shots of that.

Whereas when it gets up on the (North) Massif, it's a fairly continuous curve; although it does appear to be younger; at least there's less relief on it for the first few kilometers of that bend there (on the North Massif).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're going to have to go down like the way we came (up) because there's that big crater down at the bottom, I'm afraid.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I think we agree with that suggestion, too.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, the so-called Scarp impresses me as less of a scarp than a series of lobes which roughly have a north-south trend.
And we've been driving over various hummocks within those lobes.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think we made a gross mistake in not trying to let them get TV.
My heading hasn't changed much at all here.
They would have had a spectacular view.
Look at it out in that valley, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I still don't know where the LM is...I see it, I think.
It's the only sharp shadow out there right in the...because you sure can't make out the craters from here, can you?
Over the hill and down the rail.
(Pause) Man, I tell you, this machine is fantastic.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Likes to spin when you turn going down hill.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think you've got something right ahead of you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's that crater (at the bottom of Hole-in-the-Wall).
It doesn't look nearly as bad from here, but it sure is deep when you get up there.
I'll meander around and over this next little lobe then I'll head down that next one - the first lobe we came up - and then along it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, there's Lara, and I think we can see Station...Watch it, watch it, watch it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I'm going through it slow.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughs) I figured we'd buckle the LCRU with that one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I bet they can watch this road.
(Laughing) My heart rate just dictates the kind of terrain we're going over.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay...(Pause) Houston, we're navigating and not talking.
But the light mantle is a uniform surface and I think you've heard just about everything we have to say so far.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Your comm's great and you guys are doing (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The fragment population hasn't changed, nor has the crater population, as near as I can tell.
I hope the LRV photos will give you more details than that.
Okay, Gene, do you have the target over there, that set of...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I got to get over to this next knoll and I'm going to be off the Scarp.
We're about three-quarters of the way down.
(Pause) Isn't that sharp shadow out there the LM?
It's the only sharp shadow out there.
Right under the Sun, straight down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I'm going to try to make it down this.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This is the one we climbed up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, there's Nemo (Crater) over there to my right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yes, sir, this is the one we climbed up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The problem is any crater on the side...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't want to give us a roll...I'll use...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Keep your speed down because if you have to turn, it doesn't like it on a downhill slope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, that's got to be...(The) pitch-angle (indicator)'s pegged, and I don't know what that means.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You guys cut each other out but I take it that means you're at the edge of the Scarp.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, will you look at the hill we came down!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, I don't know, I'm impressed.
(Pause) Okay, now where we got to go?
And we want to go to (a bearing and range of) 087/6.1...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You're...I think you're headed right...right for where we want.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You can just start to see Station 3 over there now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, navigation says I've got more than 90 degrees...I should be increasing range.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: In fact, we understand it's been going so smooth down here that they haven't even spilled any coffee in the SPAN room yet this mission.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Thorson must not be on duty.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm glad we don't have any (coffee) sitting on the LCRU!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Right over there is Station 3, I think.
(Pause) I guess they would want it...Is there?...I can just start to see two craters...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You know what the problem is?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got a full planar view of the high-gain and I can't see a thing out there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: All I can do is see underneath it.
See, I can't see a lot of craters now that they're out in front.
Oh, I guess I can see them, most(ly).
Here's a nice sharp little hole; look at that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, the texture of the light mantle - (that is, the) surface texture - is really no different on the Scarp, on its flank, or out here to the east of the Scarp.
Fragment population, crater population, everything looks about the same.
If there is such a thing as a light mantle, it seems to be uniform across the Scarp.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do they look like your tracks?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, this is where we went to the big crater and I came southeast in order to get around it, remember?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Look at that big turn I made.
That was a quick change of mind when we came over that ridge.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we're still headed northwest, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, I guess one thing we don't have a handle on yet is - (and) I think we sampled them once in a Rover sample - what are the fragments out here (that are) mixed with the light mantle?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think I got one at our last gravimeter stop, a small one, and I guess there's one other Rover sample, but, at Station 3, we probably ought to make sure we get a representative suite of those fragments.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Bob, how long have we been out?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) How long have you been out?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: How long have we been out?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Three plus four five, (that is, three hours, forty-five minutes since depressurizing the cabin to below 3.5 psi).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, it certainly doesn't look like the geology of Norway, but it certainly is interesting.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That must be Lara right there, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can see the blocks on the other side of her.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I told them about those earlier.
That's the only...I think, Gene, you want to bear a little bit...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: See those two craters, two bright craters, that are just this side of Lara?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You're pointed right...almost right at them, now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I can barely see them now through that high-gain.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But I can see...I know where we're going now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Those are the two I think they wanted us to be at, and I think that's a good choice - if we can get up there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I want to get some 500s (of) the way that scarp flows up on top of...Well, it looks like it flows up on top of the North Massif.
Now it may look like the North Massif may drape material down upon it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It just doesn't look like as old a surface.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Wish they had never said anything about pictures, because I've tended to not take enough (in order) to do better.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, but Jack, you're doing quite well in the picture department...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...You're not getting too far behind or ahead.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, but I mean I'm not sure I'm getting the coverage I should.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll look at the frame counts when you get to Station 3.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Oh, there's another big crater with a pit in it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Nonchalantly laying claim to a lunar speed record) What was it, 17-1/2 or 18 clicks we hit coming down the Scarp, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't...(Laughs) I'm in Min cooling now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Wait until you get over here and look at that South Massif.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I don't know where we're going to get a good...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You know, that big block up there might be worth going to.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think that's the best station we've got right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let's see what's over on your right.
Let's see if we can get at that scarp over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've sort of lost track (of the two bright craters)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're about there (pointing at the map).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we expected you guys to be a little bit farther north...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think we want to be more to the left.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...We were guessing a heading (means "reading") of 080 for the bearing (to the LM) which really kind of says you were going a bit farther north than this.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, there's that fresh crater, there, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Realizing that what Parker has just said doesn't make sense) 080?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: All of a sudden I've lost track.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's nothing wrong with that except that...(Pause) I think we ought to go back to that big block.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Heading 080 is...Heading north is not going to...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I realize that, Gene; my mistake.
Somebody's got a wrong thing down here.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I think we need to go back there a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get up on the top, here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That was my mistake, I was reading the Hole-in-the-Wall coordinate.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: All right, Bob, we've got us some boulders over here that are in the light mantle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, now, let me brief you on Station 3.
It's going to be a very brief station to make up for the...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We can see a little bit down into Lara, now, too.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, it will be a brief station to make up for the time we added on at the...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: How'd we get up here, again?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...First of all, remember we want to get the Nav update.
Let me go into a heading of 270 more or less and give us the Nav readout so we can start that here.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) Can you get where you want to from here?
I wanted to get a high spot.
(Pause) Yeah, let's...Let me park down here, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, you should have stayed up there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I want...(Pause) This is good right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's not going to be very level for the gravimeter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gene, remember, we want to head for the west so we can get the Nav update.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Garbled) you park right out here and we can work those blocks right up behind us.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Parker) Okay, you want a Nav update here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Gene) You need to get your (low-gain) antenna.
